title: Netflix MLB Debut Filled with 'Make-Good' Ads Amid NFL Shortfalls and Ratings Struggles
body:
In a historic shift in the way we consume sports, the 2026 Major League Baseball season kicked off this week on Netflix, marking the first time a major professional sports league has partnered with a streaming giant to broadcast its games. The move is a significant milestone in the ongoing evolution of sports media, and it's no surprise that Netflix chose to debut its MLB offering with some high-profile ads from the National Football League.

The NFL, which has historically been a major player in the world of sports broadcasting, has faced some notable shortfalls in recent years, including declining viewership numbers and increased competition from other streaming services. As a result, the league has been forced to rethink its approach to marketing and advertising, and it appears that Netflix has taken advantage of this shift by incorporating "make-good" ads into its MLB broadcasts. These ads, which are essentially additional commercials that the NFL has purchased in the hopes of recouping some of the lost revenue from its declining viewership numbers, will undoubtedly be a major part of the Netflix MLB experience.

The addition of these ads has sparked some debate among sports fans and industry observers, with some expressing concern that they may detract from the overall viewing experience. However, it's worth noting that the NFL has a long history of using innovative marketing tactics to reach its audience, and it's possible that these ads could be seen as a bold experiment in the world of sports broadcasting. After all, the NFL has always been known for its ability to think outside the box and push the boundaries of what's possible in the world of sports marketing.

Despite the controversy surrounding the "make-good" ads, the Netflix MLB partnership is a significant development for fans of the sport. For years, MLB has struggled to gain traction in the world of sports broadcasting, and this partnership represents a major opportunity for the league to reach a new and wider audience. With Netflix's vast resources and global reach, it's possible that the league could see a significant spike in viewership numbers, which would be a major boost for the sport as a whole.

As the 2026 MLB season gets underway on Netflix, it will be fascinating to see how the partnership between the league and the streaming giant plays out. Will the addition of "make-good" ads from the NFL prove to be a hit with fans, or will they detract from the overall viewing experience? Only time will tell, but one thing is certain: the future of sports broadcasting is looking more exciting than ever. With the likes of Netflix, Amazon, and other streaming services vying for a piece of the action, it's clear that the traditional model of sports broadcasting is no longer enough. The game is changing, and it will be fascinating to see how the major sports leagues adapt to this new reality. As the 2026 MLB season gets underway on Netflix, one thing is certain: this is just the beginning of a new era in sports broadcasting.
